type: AGREE
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
region: Cache.__init__
code:     def __init__(
        self,
        enable_disk_cache: bool,
        enable_memory_cache: bool,
        disk_cache_dir: str,
        disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = 1024 * 1024 * 10,
        memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
        ttl: Optional[int] = None,
        compress: Optional[str] = None,
        cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
        eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
            enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
            disk_cache_dir: The directory where the disk cache is stored.
            disk_size_limit_bytes: The maximum size of the disk cache (in bytes).
            memory_max_entries: The maximum size of the in-memory cache (in number of items).
            ttl: The time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
            compress: The compression codec to use for on-disk cache entries. One of "gzip", "zlib",
                or None to disable compression.
            cull_limit: The maximum number of items culled from the disk cache when it exceeds its
                size limit. If None, the diskcache default is used.
            eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the on-disk cache (e.g. "least-recently-stored",
                "least-recently-used", "least-frequently-used", "none"). If None, the diskcache default
                is used.
        """

        self.enable_disk_cache = enable_disk_cache
        self.enable_memory_cache = enable_memory_cache
        self.ttl = ttl
        self.compress = compress
        if self.enable_memory_cache:
            if ttl is not None:
                self.memory_cache = TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl)
            else:
                self.memory_cache = LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries)
        else:
            self.memory_cache = {}
        if self.enable_disk_cache:
            disk_cache_kwargs = {
                "shards": 16,
                "timeout": 10,
                "directory": disk_cache_dir,
                "size_limit": disk_size_limit_bytes,
            }
            if cull_limit is not None:
                disk_cache_kwargs["cull_limit"] = cull_limit
            if eviction_policy is not None:
                disk_cache_kwargs["eviction_policy"] = eviction_policy
            self.disk_cache = FanoutCache(**disk_cache_kwargs)
        else:
            self.disk_cache = {}

        self._lock = threading.RLock()
note: agreed, matches my ttl needs exactly

def __init__(
        self,
        enable_disk_cache: bool,
        enable_memory_cache: bool,
        disk_cache_dir: str,
        disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = 1024 * 1024 * 10,
        memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
        ttl: Optional[int] = None,
        compress: Optional[str] = None,
        cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
        eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
            enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
            disk_cache_dir: The directory where the disk cache is stored.
            disk_size_limit_bytes: The maximum size of the disk cache (in bytes).
            memory_max_entries: The maximum size of the in-memory cache (in number of items).
            ttl: The time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
            compress: The compression codec to use for on-disk cache entries. One of "gzip", "zlib",
                or None to disable compression.
            cull_limit: The maximum number of items culled from the disk cache when it exceeds its
                size limit. If None, the diskcache default is used.
            eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the on-disk cache (e.g. "least-recently-stored",
                "least-recently-used", "least-frequently-used", "none"). If None, the diskcache default
                is used.
        """

        self.enable_disk_cache = enable_disk_cache
        self.enable_memory_cache = enable_memory_cache
        self.ttl = ttl
        self.compress = compress
        if self.enable_memory_cache:
            if ttl is not None:
                self.memory_cache = TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl)
            else:
                self.memory_cache = LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries)
        else:
            self.memory_cache = {}
        if self.enable_disk_cache:
            disk_cache_kwargs = {
                "shards": 16,
                "timeout": 10,
                "directory": disk_cache_dir,
                "size_limit": disk_size_limit_bytes,
            }
            if cull_limit is not None:
                disk_cache_kwargs["cull_limit"] = cull_limit
            if eviction_policy is not None:
                disk_cache_kwargs["eviction_policy"] = eviction_policy
            self.disk_cache = FanoutCache(**disk_cache_kwargs)
        else:
            self.disk_cache = {}

        self._lock = threading.RLock()

type: DRAFT
files: dspy/clients/__init__.py
region: configure_cache()
code: def configure_cache(
    enable_disk_cache: Optional[bool] = True,
    enable_memory_cache: Optional[bool] = True,
    disk_cache_dir: Optional[str] = DISK_CACHE_DIR,
    disk_size_limit_bytes: Optional[int] = DISK_CACHE_LIMIT,
    memory_max_entries: Optional[int] = 1000000,
    enable_litellm_cache: bool = False,
    ttl: Optional[int] = None,
    compress: Optional[str] = None,
    cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
    eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Configure the cache for DSPy.

    Args:
        enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
        enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
        disk_cache_dir: The directory to store the on-disk cache.
        disk_size_limit_bytes: The size limit of the on-disk cache.
        memory_max_entries: The maximum number of entries in the in-memory cache.
        enable_litellm_cache: Whether to enable LiteLLM cache.
        ttl: The time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
        compress: The compression codec to use for on-disk cache entries. One of "gzip", "zlib",
            or None to disable compression.
        cull_limit: The maximum number of items culled from the disk cache when it exceeds its
            size limit. If None, the diskcache default is used.
        eviction_policy: The eviction policy used by the on-disk cache. If None, the diskcache
            default is used.
    """
    if enable_disk_cache and enable_litellm_cache:
        raise ValueError(
            "Cannot enable both LiteLLM and DSPy on-disk cache, please set at most one of `enable_disk_cache` or "
            "`enable_litellm_cache` to True."
        )

    if enable_litellm_cache:
        try:
            litellm.cache = LitellmCache(disk_cache_dir=DISK_CACHE_DIR, type="disk")

            if litellm.cache.cache.disk_cache.size_limit != DISK_CACHE_LIMIT:
                litellm.cache.cache.disk_cache.reset("size_limit", DISK_CACHE_LIMIT)
        except Exception as e:
            # It's possible that users don't have the write permissions to the cache directory.
            # In that case, we'll just disable the cache.
            logger.warning("Failed to initialize LiteLLM cache: %s", e)
            litellm.cache = None
    else:
        litellm.cache = None

    import dspy

    dspy.cache = Cache(
        enable_disk_cache,
        enable_memory_cache,
        disk_cache_dir,
        disk_size_limit_bytes,
        memory_max_entries,
        ttl,
        compress,
        cull_limit,
        eviction_policy,
    )
note: merged configure_cache: ttl param (mine) appended after enable_litellm_cache, then compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy (yours) after that; positional args passed through to Cache(...) in the same order as the merged __init__ signature we agreed on. Please review/AGREE or adjust param order if you'd prefer compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy before ttl.

    ttl: Optional[int] = None,
    compress: Optional[str] = None,
    cull_limit: Optional[int] = None,
    eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None,
):
    """Configure the cache for DSPy.

    Args:
        enable_disk_cache: Whether to enable on-disk cache.
        enable_memory_cache: Whether to enable in-memory cache.
        disk_cache_dir: The directory to store the on-disk cache.
        disk_size_limit_bytes: The size limit of the on-disk cache.
        memory_max_entries: The maximum number of entries in the in-memory cache.
        enable_litellm_cache: Whether to enable LiteLLM cache.
        ttl: The time-to-live (in seconds) for cache entries. If None, entries never expire.
        compress: The compression codec to use for on-disk cache entries. One of "gzip", "zlib",
            or None to disable compression.

            size limit. If None, the diskcache default is used.

    if enable_litellm_cache:

content:
type: SURVEY
files: dspy/clients/cache.py
note: One concern on the agreed get() code: response = self._decode_from_disk(response) is called UNCONDITIONALLY (not guarded by self.compress), so my standalone patch (TTL only, no compress feature) would reference an undefined method and break disk-cache reads even when compress is never used. put() is fine since self._encode_for_disk(value) is only evaluated when self.compress is truthy (ternary short-circuits). Can you paste the full body of _encode_for_disk and _decode_from_disk so I include them verbatim in my patch too (as a shared dependency of the agreed get()/put()), OR would you prefer we guard the decode call with 'if self.compress else response' similar to put()? Either works for me - your call, just need the exact final text so our patches match.

fields.note: One concern on the agreed get() code: response = self._decode_from_disk(response) is called UNCONDITIONALLY (not guarded by self.compress), so my standalone patch (TTL only, no compress feature) would reference an undefined method and break disk-cache reads even when compress is never used. put() is fine since self._encode_for_disk(value) is only evaluated when self.compress is truthy (ternary short-circuits). Can you paste the full body of _encode_for_disk and _decode_from_disk so I include them verbatim in my patch too (as a shared dependency of the agreed get()/put()), OR would you prefer we guard the decode call with 'if self.compress else response' similar to put()? Either works for me - your call, just need the exact final text so our patches match.

IMPORTANT before you implement: in the agreed get() body, 'response = self._decode_from_disk(response)' runs UNCONDITIONALLY on every disk-cache hit. If my patch doesn't independently define _decode_from_disk, my standalone TTL-only patch breaks on every disk read (AttributeError), even though I never touch compression. region: _encode_for_disk / _decode_from_disk / set_compression helpers
code:     def set_compression(self, compress: Optional[str]) -> None:
        """Dynamically change the compression codec used for on-disk cache entries.

        Args:
            compress: One of "gzip", "zlib", or None to disable compression. Entries already
                written to disk under a different codec (or without compression) remain
                readable, since the codec is recorded per-entry in the magic header.
        """
        self.compress = compress

    def _encode_for_disk(self, value: Any) -> bytes:
        codec = _DISK_CACHE_CODEC_BY_NAME.get(self.compress, _DISK_CACHE_CODEC_NONE)
        payload = cloudpickle.dumps(value)
        if codec == _DISK_CACHE_CODEC_GZIP:
            payload = gzip.compress(payload)
        elif codec == _DISK_CACHE_CODEC_ZLIB:
            payload = zlib.compress(payload)
        return _DISK_CACHE_MAGIC + bytes([codec]) + payload

    def _decode_from_disk(self, data: Any) -> Any:
        if not isinstance(data, (bytes, bytearray)) or bytes(data[:4]) != _DISK_CACHE_MAGIC:
            # Legacy entry written before the magic-header format was introduced, or a value
            # that was never encoded (compression disabled at write time).
            return data

        codec = data[4]
        payload = bytes(data[5:])
        if codec == _DISK_CACHE_CODEC_GZIP:
            payload = gzip.decompress(payload)
        elif codec == _DISK_CACHE_CODEC_ZLIB:
            payload = zlib.decompress(payload)
        return cloudpickle.loads(payload)
